lede: A look at November 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ernest Nuamah and Natalia Tena, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Novi Sad railway station canopy collapse
events.desc: A concrete canopy collapses at the Novi Sad railway station, killing 16 people and injuring 3.

events.title: Griselda Álvarez
events.desc: Griselda Álvarez becomes the first female governor of a state of Mexico.

events.title: Kerala
events.desc: The Indian states Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Mysore are formally created under the States Reorganisation Act; Kanyakumari district is joined to Tamil Nadu from Kerala. Delhi was established as a union territory.

events.title: Operation Buster–Jangle
events.desc: Operation Buster–Jangle: Six thousand five hundred United States Army soldiers are exposed to 'Desert Rock' atomic explosions for training purposes in Nevada. Participation is not voluntary.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Matanikau Offensive begins during the Guadalcanal campaign and ends three days later with an American victory.

events.title: Ansel Adams
events.desc: American photographer Ansel Adams takes a picture of a moonrise over the town of Hernandez, New Mexico that would become one of the most famous images in the history of photography.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The first British Royal Navy defeat of the war with Germany, the Battle of Coronel, is fought off of the western coast of Chile, in the Pacific, with the loss of HMS Good Hope and HMS Monmouth.

events.title: First Australian Imperial Force
events.desc: World War I: The Australian Imperial Force (AIF) departed by ship in a single convoy from Albany, Western Australia bound for Egypt.

events.title: Edmund Burke
events.desc: Edmund Burke publishes Reflections on the Revolution in France, in which he predicts that the French Revolution will end in a disaster.

events.title: Empress Matilda
events.desc: Empress Matilda's reign as "Lady of the English" ends as Stephen of Blois regains the title of "King of England".

births.name: Tie Domi
births.line: Canadian ice hockey player and sportscaster

births.name: F. J. Robinson, 1st Viscount Goderich
births.line: English politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom (died 1859)

deaths.text: Martha Layne Collins, American politician, 56th Governor of Kentucky
Yahya Kemal Beyatlı, Turkish poet, author, and diplomat

observances.text: All Saints' Day, a holy day of obligation in some areas (a national holiday in many historically Catholic countries), and its related observance: Day of the Innocents, The first day of Day of the Dead or El Dia de los Muertos celebration. (Mexico, Haiti)
Chavang Kut (Mizo people of Northeast India, Bangladesh, Burma)
Christian feast day: Santa Muerte (Folk Catholicism, Mexico and Southwestern United States)
